authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.40(b)(55) — Little River (Humboldt Co.) downstream from the County Road bridge at Crannell
sources.quotedText: (55) Little River (Humboldt Co.) downstream from the County Road bridge at Crannell, excluding tributaries. Fourth Sat. in May through Mar. 31. Cutthroat Trout minimum size limit: 10 inches total length. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used from the fourth Sat. in May through Oct. 31. Only barbless hooks may be used from Nov. 1 through Mar. 31. 2 Cutthroat Trout. 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

sources.title: CDFW — Fishing for Coastal Cutthroat Trout
sources.quotedText: Found in Humboldt Bay tributaries, numerous lagoons and ponds, and large stream systems such as the Smith, Mad, and lower Klamath rivers.

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

parameters.note: Applies to Little River downstream from the County Road bridge at Crannell, excluding tributaries.

regulations.humanSummary: Open fourth Saturday in May through March 31
regulations.verbatimText: Fourth Sat. in May through Mar. 31.

regulations.humanSummary: Cutthroat Trout minimum size limit of 10 inches total length
regulations.verbatimText: Cutthroat Trout minimum size limit: 10 inches total length.

parameters.note: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used from the fourth Saturday in May through October 31.

regulations.humanSummary: Fourth Saturday in May through October 31: only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used
regulations.verbatimText: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used from the fourth Sat. in May through Oct. 31.

parameters.note: Only barbless hooks may be used from November 1 through March 31 (bait permitted if hook is barbless).

regulations.humanSummary: November 1 through March 31: only barbless hooks may be used
regulations.verbatimText: Only barbless hooks may be used from Nov. 1 through Mar. 31.

parameters.counting_rule: 2 Cutthroat Trout daily bag limit, counted separately from the hatchery trout/steelhead limit; no separate possession figure is stated for Cutthroat Trout so the daily figure applies.
2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead per day, combined; 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead in possession, combined. Counted separately from the Cutthroat Trout limit.

regulations.humanSummary: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead daily bag limit; 4 in possession
regulations.verbatimText: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
